examples: Steam condenses into water when it cools.
There the air absorbs more moisture, which then condenses on a metal wall cooled by seawater, and thus distilled water for irrigating the plants collects.
Water vapour condenses to form clouds.
As the air rises, it expands and cools, and water vapour condenses, releasing even more heat.
That condenses it back into a liquid, and the whole process can be repeated.
